# Pragmatic Programming — F5

> The F5 chapter overview: a pragmatic build of the Portal engine in Elixir. Nine modules carry one product from a decoupled core through domain modeling, a tracer-bullet walking skeleton, design by contract, commands/queries/events, where state lives, testing, and integration seams — ending in a lab where the engine facade is mounted behind a LiveView sketch, ready to integrate with Phoenix LiveView in F6.

Chapter map for LLMs/agents. Hub: [/elixir/pragmatic](/elixir/pragmatic). Links are clean root-relative URLs; this file lists every lesson in the chapter in course order.

## Lessons

- [The Portal engine blueprint — F5.0.1](/elixir/pragmatic/architecture): The system this chapter builds, at a glance: the Portal engine is a framework-free domain core that sits between the branded CHAMP store from F4 below and the Phoenix…
- [The domain model — F5.0.2](/elixir/pragmatic/domain-model): The data the Portal engine owns: three bounded contexts — Accounts (User, Session), Catalog (Course, Lesson, Page), and Learning (Enrollment, Progress) — modeled as…
- [The command & event flow — F5.0.3](/elixir/pragmatic/flow): How one use case moves through the engine: a command is checked against a contract, emits a domain event, the event transitions state, and a query reads a projection.…
- [Start thin: a running Portal from day one — F5.01](/elixir/pragmatic/foundations): Pragmatic programming starts with a system that runs. F5.01 stands the Portal up behind a minimal Elixir web server from the first day, places that move on a course-wide…
  - [The development roadmap — F5.01.1](/elixir/pragmatic/foundations/roadmap): The whole course is one development roadmap: HTML templating, then a simple web server, then the Portal logic behind it, then Phoenix in F6, then Fly in production. You…
  - [A thin web server in Elixir — F5.01.2](/elixir/pragmatic/foundations/thin-server): A minimal HTTP front end for the Portal: a Plug.Router matched and dispatched by Bandit, where each route does one thing — turn the request into a command or a query…
  - [A web layer built for replacement — F5.01.3](/elixir/pragmatic/foundations/replaceable): The thin server is a detail, by design. Because every route only calls Portal.Engine.dispatch/1 and query/2, the same calls move unchanged into a Phoenix controller or a…
- [Modeling the Portal domain — F5.02](/elixir/pragmatic/domain): The engine needs a shape before it needs behavior. F5.02 models the Portal's domain in three layers: each entity is a plain struct with a typespec; entities are grouped…
  - [Structs & typespecs — F5.02.1](/elixir/pragmatic/domain/structs): An entity is a plain struct: @enforce_keys names the fields it cannot exist without, defstruct gives the optional ones their defaults, and a @type t documents the shape…
  - [Bounded contexts — F5.02.2](/elixir/pragmatic/domain/contexts): A bounded context is a module that owns a few entities and guards their rules — Accounts owns User and Session, Catalog owns Course and Lesson, Learning owns Enrollment…
  - [A context's public API — F5.02.3](/elixir/pragmatic/domain/api): Each context exposes a small set of public functions — a smart constructor that validates and returns {:ok, struct} or {:error, reason}, a command, a query — and keeps…
- [Tracer bullets: a walking skeleton — F5.03](/elixir/pragmatic/tracer-bullets): With a running server and a domain model in hand, F5.03 wires them together by driving one use case — enroll a learner — through every layer at once: route, context API…
  - [Tracer bullets vs prototypes — F5.03.1](/elixir/pragmatic/tracer-bullets/prototypes): Both are built fast, but their fates are opposite. A tracer bullet is thin but real code that round-trips the whole system and is kept and built upon; a prototype is…
  - [The walking skeleton — F5.03.2](/elixir/pragmatic/tracer-bullets/skeleton): The enroll-a-learner slice, end to end: a POST /enroll route calls Learning.enroll/2, which builds an %Enrollment{} and puts it in the store, and the handler answers…
  - [Iterating the slice — F5.03.3](/elixir/pragmatic/tracer-bullets/iterating): Once the skeleton walks, you grow it one thin vertical slice at a time: deliver the first lesson, then record progress, each slice touching route, context, struct, and…
- [Design by contract — F5.04](/elixir/pragmatic/contracts): Every command on the engine carries a contract: a precondition the caller must meet, a postcondition the function guarantees, and an invariant always true of the state.…
  - [Preconditions, postconditions & invariants — F5.04.1](/elixir/pragmatic/contracts/conditions): A contract has three parts and three owners. The precondition is the caller's obligation — valid ids, not already enrolled. The postcondition is the function's guarantee…
  - [Assertions in Elixir — F5.04.2](/elixir/pragmatic/contracts/assertions): Elixir has no design-by-contract keywords, so contracts are written in its idioms: guards and pattern matching express preconditions on shape, a with chain composes them…
  - [Failing fast — F5.04.3](/elixir/pragmatic/contracts/fail-fast): Check at the boundary and stop on the first violation, before the struct is built or the store is touched. An expected failure returns a tagged error the caller handles…
- [Commands, queries & events — F5.05](/elixir/pragmatic/cqrs): With the enroll command now contract-checked, F5.05 formalizes how the engine handles change: writes are commands that return only success or failure, reads are queries…
  - [Command/query separation — F5.05.1](/elixir/pragmatic/cqrs/cqs): Command/query separation is one rule: a function either changes state and returns only whether it worked, or returns data and changes nothing — never both. enroll is a…
  - [Domain events — F5.05.2](/elixir/pragmatic/cqrs/events): Model every change as a past-tense fact: %LearnerEnrolled{}, %LessonDelivered{}, %ProgressRecorded{}. An event is immutable, named for what happened, and carries the…
  - [The engine as a reducer — F5.05.3](/elixir/pragmatic/cqrs/reducer): State is not stored so much as derived: a command emits events, each event evolves the state, and one reduce over the event log replays the engine to its current state.…
- [Where engine state lives — F5.06](/elixir/pragmatic/state): The F5.05 fold is pure: it computes the current state from the event log and forgets it. At runtime the Portal must keep that state alive between requests, and on the…
  - [Choosing where state lives — F5.06.1](/elixir/pragmatic/state/choosing): Three holders for live state on the BEAM are not interchangeable: a GenServer is a process that holds state and runs logic, an Agent holds a value with get and update…
  - [The engine GenServer — F5.06.2](/elixir/pragmatic/state/genserver): Three callbacks carry the engine. init folds the event log into the starting state; a command handle_call runs decide and evolve and keeps the new state; a query…
  - [Supervision — F5.06.3](/elixir/pragmatic/state/supervision): Let it crash: a process holding state will eventually fail, so a supervisor sits above the engine and restarts it. On restart init folds the event log again and the…
- [Pragmatic testing — F5.07](/elixir/pragmatic/testing): The engine was built to be tested: decide and evolve are pure and state is a fold, so most of it is checked with plain example tests — no processes, no mocks. Above…
  - [Testing the pure core — F5.07.1](/elixir/pragmatic/testing/pure-core): decide, evolve, and replay are pure functions, so a test is three lines: arrange a state, call the function, assert the output — no process to start, no database to…
  - [Property-based testing — F5.07.2](/elixir/pragmatic/testing/property): State a property true for every valid input and let StreamData generate hundreds of cases, shrinking any failure to the smallest counterexample. The engine's properties…
  - [Contract tests — F5.07.3](/elixir/pragmatic/testing/contract-tests): The F5.04 contract written as tests: a precondition test feeds a bad command and asserts {:error, reason} with no state change, a postcondition test asserts the…
- [Boundaries & integration seams — F5.08](/elixir/pragmatic/boundaries): The engine works, but its callers still have to know it is a process with message shapes and a chosen store. F5.08 draws the boundary with hexagonal architecture: the…
  - [Ports & adapters — F5.08.1](/elixir/pragmatic/boundaries/ports): A port is an Elixir behaviour the core depends on; an adapter is a module that implements it; configuration decides which adapter is loaded. One EventStore contract, an…
  - [The engine facade — F5.08.2](/elixir/pragmatic/boundaries/facade): The driving port: a small context module — enroll/2, deliver_lesson/2, progress_of/1 — that names intentions and hides the GenServer, the event log, and the reducer. It…
  - [Error contracts for the UI — F5.08.3](/elixir/pragmatic/boundaries/errors): Failure is part of the contract: a closed set of %Portal.Error{} codes, mapped from internal reasons at the boundary, so the UI renders a finite list of outcomes…
- [Lab: the Portal engine, LiveView-ready — F5.09](/elixir/pragmatic/engine-lab): The finale assembles eight modules into one running Portal: a supervision tree with the engine behind its facade, fed by an event-store port, mounted behind a LiveView…
  - [The engine facade end to end — F5.09.1](/elixir/pragmatic/engine-lab/end-to-end): The full supervision tree: Portal.Application starts the configured store adapter and the engine; the engine's init reads the whole stream through the port and replays…
  - [A LiveView mount sketch — F5.09.2](/elixir/pragmatic/engine-lab/mount): A LiveView that touches only the facade: mount/3 loads state with a query, handle_event/3 issues a command and branches on the closed error contract, and render/1 draws…
  - [What ships in F6 — F5.09.3](/elixir/pragmatic/engine-lab/handoff): The handoff: F6 replaces the thin web layer with Phoenix and adds its endpoint to the same supervision tree, but the Portal facade is called unchanged, the closed…
